news.title: World Cup 2026: Spain midfielder says Argentina players should not be punished by FIFA after post-match scuffle
news.lede: Tensions boiled over following Spain's 1-0 win over Argentina in the World Cup final. Moments after the match ended, Argentina's Leandro Paredes grabbed Spain's Eric García by the throat. After shoving García to the ground, Paredes turned his attention to Spain's Gavi. A day later, FIFA announced it would investigate Argentina for its actions following the loss.

news.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
news.lede: Fight breaks out between Spain and Argentina players at full-time. Spain midfielder Gavi does not believe Argentina's Leandro Paredes should be suspended for his part in the confrontation following the World Cup final. Argentina players Paredes, Nahuel Molina, Thiago Almada and assistant coach Roberto Ayala all appeared to be involved in the fracas on the pitch as Spain celebrated their 1-0 win. Paredes seemed to push Spain's Eric Garcia by the throat and unused substitute Gavi tried to intervene but was forced to the ground by Almada.

news.title: World Cup 2030 (very early) power rankings: who will challenge Spain at top?
news.lede: The next men’s World Cup is still four years away, but we take a look at the teams with the firmest foundations who should be primed to contend. The 2026 World Cup provided many an esteemed veteran with what could be their international swansongs, with golden greats Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, Luka Modric, Neymar, James Rodríguez and Guillermo Ochoa all making teary exits as their tournament ended. As we turn ahead to the 2030 World Cup, any player 32 or older will be sweating their selection status and changing their daily routines to stay sharp against inevitable aging. For these power rankings, we’re overvaluing sure things.

news.title: Shakira Leads FIFA’s Historic First World Cup Final Halftime Show
news.lede: Shakira made history on July 19 by headlining FIFA’s first-ever World Cup final halftime show, placing Colombia at the center of one of football’s biggest global spectacles. Performing at MetLife Stadium alongside international stars including Madonna, BTS, and Justin Bieber, the Colombian singer delivered “Dai Dai,” the official anthem of FIFA’s Global Education Fund, during a landmark production that combined music, football, and philanthropy. The performance marked a historic first for the men’s FIFA World Cup, introducing a Super Bowl-style halftime show designed to celebrate both the sport and its growing cultural influence around the world.

news.title: England cannot afford trip to amputee World Cup – and the FA will not help
news.lede: With no national funding, England’s successful men’s amputee side is struggling to raise the £50,000 needed for November’s tournament in Mexico. Every time Scott Rogers walks into St George’s Park there is a sign on the wall that catches his eye. It boasts that the 330-acre site is the proud home of all England’s football teams. “I look at it every time and go, ‘Well, not all of them’,” Rogers says.

news.title: FIFA prosecutor has already punished Argentina for World Cup final antics with instant penalty
news.lede: Argentina have already faced a hefty fine for their actions after winning the World Cup in 2022 . Much talk has centred on what punishment La Albiceleste would face for the wild scenes after this year's final , with FIFA's disciplinary and ethics prosecutor appointed to review events. Yet the nation has been on the receiving end of a FIFA prosecution once before. Argentina were slammed for the way they celebrated after beating France via spot-kicks in Qatar in December four years ago.

news.title: Where is the next World Cup 2030? Which countries are hosting?
news.lede: This content is provided by Exco Player and may use cookies or similar technologies. Please click 'Allow and Continue' below to load the content. The 2030 World Cup will be unlike any previous edition of football's biggest tournament. For the first time in the competition's history, matches will be staged across three continents, with six countries sharing hosting duties to celebrate the centenary of the World Cup.

news.title: Chattanooga local celebrating Spain’s World Cup victory
news.lede: Spain is celebrating with all their might after their World Cup win over Argentina, and one Chattanoogan had the chance to celebrate overseas with the Spaniards. Jack Summerlin is a Chattanooga native and currently studies at the University of Alabama. He is studying abroad in Spain this summer and has been able to experience the country’s celebrations throughout the tournament. His mother, Scottie Summerlin, joined him abroad just when Spain won the World Cup championship.
